content:
# 10-Bit Pipeline Design Decision (2026-04-02)

## Decision
Upgrade Dorea pipeline from 8-bit to 10-bit I/O (Approach A: 10-bit I/O, f32 internal) to support both DJI Action 4 D-Log M (10-bit H.265) and Insta360 X5 I-Log (10-bit ProRes 422 HQ). DaVinci Resolve owns final SDR tone mapping and 8-bit conversion.

## Key Design Elements

### Camera Input Abstraction
- Trait-based `TransferFunction` with implementations: DLogM, ILog, Srgb, LutBased
- I-Log starts as S-Log3 proxy; empirical extraction deferred (shoot grayscale chart, fit curve, drop into 1D .cube LutBased variant)
- Camera-aware shoulder rolloff: D-Log M=0.85, I-Log=0.88, sRGB=0.95
- `ClipMeta` struct with encoding, projection (Rectilinear|TinyPlanet), source_bit_depth, container_hint
- Auto-detection via ffprobe with mismatch warnings
- ProRes is all-intra: frame selection skips I-frame filter

### 16-Bit I/O
- ffmpeg extracts keyframes as 16-bit PNG (rgb48be) instead of JPEG
- `load_rgb_image()` uses `.into_rgb16()` / 65535.0
- `inference.rs` PNG codec supports both 8-bit and 16-bit
- LUT size: 33^3 -> 65^3 for 10-bit input (configurable, auto-defaulting)

### Performance Architecture
- 3-stage pipelined frame loop: decode -> depth inference -> grade+encode (bounded channels, cap 3)
- Inference stays 8-bit proxy (depth model doesn't benefit from 16-bit)
- Persistent GPU LUT allocation (upload 16.5 MB once, not per-frame)
- CUDA kernels: u8 -> f32 signature
- Batched depth inference (2-3 frames per IPC call)
- NVDEC for H.265 10-bit decode; CPU for ProRes
- Output: ProRes 422 HQ 10-bit (default) or HEVC 10-bit NVENC

### Tiny Planet
- Skip depth estimation (model fails on stereographic projection)
- Use zone 0 LUT with uniform depth=0.5
- 2-stage pipeline (no Python subprocess), expected >15 fps
- Heuristic warning for degenerate depth maps

### Insta360 X5 Workflow
- Shoot I-Log 5.7K HDR
- Export from Insta360 Studio: Color Plus OFF, no LUTs, ProRes 422 HQ
- Reframe to flat perspective (wide or tiny planet)
- Verify: exported clip should look flat/washed out in Resolve (confirms I-Log preserved)

## VRAM Budget
Peak ~2.8 GB (depth batch=2) on RTX 3060 6GB. Stages 2 and 3 never share GPU simultaneously.

## Full spec
See `docs/decisions/2026-04-02-10bit-pipeline-design.md`
